case_title: MEGHAN J. LEVINS VS. LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC. ET AL

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS ACTION ON GROUND OF INCONVENIENT FORUM TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100012051) FILED BY DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS ACTION ON GROUND OF INCONVENIENT FORUM TO PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. BASED ON A WEIGHING OF THE PRIVATE AND PUBLIC FACTORS, THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS. (SEE STANGVIK V. SHILEY INC. (1991) 54 CAL.3D 744.) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT, HER CHOICE OF FORUM IS GIVEN SUBSTANTIAL WEIGHT IN BALANCING THE PRIVATE AND PUBLIC FACTORS. (STANGVIK V. SHILEY INC. (1991) 54 CAL.3D 744, 751.) ALSO, CALIFORNIA HAS AN INTEREST IN PROVIDING ITS RESIDENTS WITH A FORUM IN WHICH THEY CAN SEEK REDRESS FOR THEIR GRIEVANCES. ALTHOUGH THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED IN UTAH, PLAINTIFF'S CONTACTS WITH DEFENDANT, THROUGH ITS SUBSIDIARY TEAM IN TRAINING, OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA, PLAINTIFF ALLEGEDLY ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT REGARDING HER FUNDRAISING PARTICIPATION IN CALIFORNIA, AND PLAINTIFF'S TRAINING FOR THE EVENT OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE WHICH ESTABLISHES THAT IT WAS AN INTENDED BENEFICIARY OF THE SKINNY TIRE EVENTS, LLC'S WAIVER AND THERE IS NO INDICATION THAT THE SUPERIOR COURT IN UTAH FOUND THAT THE WAIVER COVERED DEFENDANT. ADDITIONALLY, THE PROVISION OF THE SKINNY TIRE EVENTS, LLC WAIVER FORM WHICH DEFENDANT CITES TO IS A GOVERNING LAW CLAUSE AND IS NOT A FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT STATE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. (CCP SECTION 430.10(E).) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT OWED HER A DUTY. AS DEFENDANT CORRECTLY NOTES, PLAINTIFF IS REQUESTING THAT THE COURT DEDUCE THAT DEFENDANT HAD A DUTY TO INSPECT, WARN, AND ENSURE THE EXISTENCE OF SUFFICIENT EMERGENCY RESPONSE METHODS BASED OFF OF THE FACT THAT THE RIDE WAS HELD FOR THE BENEFIT OF DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE THE BASIS FOR DEFENDANT'S DUTY TO INSPECT, WARN, AND ENSURE THE EXISTENCE OF SUFFICIENT EMERGENCY RESPONSE METHODS. THE DOCTRINE OF EXCLUSIVE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION DOES NOT APPLY TO THE CASE AT HAND. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-14-2017 Continued To Nov-20-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Malek, Esq. (310-540-5100) of Malek & Malek for Plaintiff.
Heather Ann Young, Esq. (707-525-8800) of Perry Johnson Anderson Miller & Moskowitz for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether LLS acted with gross negligence. "Gross negligence" is a "want of scant care" or "an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct." (City of Santa Barbara v. Sup. Crt. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754; CACI 425.) In this case, LLS presents no evidence that anyone ever inspected the bike route prior to the event. (LLS' UMF 1-34.) Such a failure could constitute "an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct." LLS contends that Skinny Tires, LLC-not LLS-was responsible for picking and inspecting the bike route. (Larsen Dec. pars. 8-9.) There is a triable issue of material fact regarding this issue. Plaintiff highlights LLS's "Team In Training Staff and Coaches Meeting Agenda" (9/17/13) that indicates that LLS would "[s]cout out assignments today" and "[o]ne team designated to sweep the course after they have finished their assignments." (Lewis Dec., Ex. 7.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that LLS did in fact have a role in inspecting the course and acted with gross negligence by failing to do so. LLS fails to show that primary assumption of the risk bars plaintiff's claim. Under Saffro v. Elite Racing, Inc. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 173, 179, a race organizer "has a duty to organize and conduct a reasonably safe event, which requires it to 'minimize the risks without altering the nature of the sport.'" LLS' alleged failure to inspect the course prior to the race would not alter the nature of the sport. There is a triable issue of material fact whether plaintiff released her claims based on the Team in Training and Moab Century Tour releases. A release does not apply to a claim for gross negligence. (See Rosencrans v. Dover Images., Ltd. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1072, 1081.) Because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether LLS acted with gross negligence, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the releases bar the claim. Plaintiff's argument that the release violates public policy because LLS is a nonprofit and the public interest is implicated lacks merit. (See Tunkl v. Regents of the University of California (1963) 60 Cal.2d 92, 96.) This case does not raise a Tunkl issue, e.g., hospital attempting to rely on a release to bar suit over improper medical care. Plaintiff's argument that the release expired by its own terms one year after the event is also inapposite. The claim accrued within the release period. The Moab Century Tour release is broad enough to apply to LLS because it also refers to agents, representatives, sponsors, etc. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/rbu)

Notice Of Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum To Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Malek of Malek & Malek Law Offices telephone number 415-981-0848 for Plaintiff.
Heather-Ann Young of Perry Johnson Anderson Miller & Moskowitz, LLP telephone number 707-525-8800 appeared by telephone number for Defendant Leukemia & Lymphoma Society, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC'S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum To Plaintiffs Complaint is denied. Based on a weighing of the private and public factors, the court denies Defendant's motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens. (See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744.) Because Plaintiff is a California resident, her choice of forum is given substantial weight in balancing the private and public factors. (Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751.) Also, California has an interest in providing its residents with a forum in which they can seek redress for their grievances. Although the accident occurred in Utah, Plaintiff's contacts with Defendant, through its subsidiary Team in Training, occurred in California, Plaintiff allegedly entered into an agreement with Defendant regarding her fundraising participation in California, and Plaintiff's training for the event occurred in California. Defendant has not presented evidence which establishes that it was an intended beneficiary of the Skinny Tire Events, LLC's waiver and there is no indication that the superior court in Utah found that the waiver covered Defendant. Additionally, the provision of the Skinny Tire Events, LLC waiver form which Defendant cites to is a governing law clause and is not a forum selection clause. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Malek of Malek & Malek Law Offices telephone number 415-981-0848 for Plaintiff.
Heather-Ann Young of Perry Johnson Anderson Miller & Moskowitz, LLP telephone number 707-525-8800 appeared by telephone number for Defendant Leukemia & Lymphoma Society, Inc.

Ms. Young stated that she is only arguing the motion to stay or dismiss action. The Court modified its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEUKEMIA & LYMPHOMA SOCIETY, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is sustained with thirty days leave to amend. The complaint does not state sufficient facts to support the negligence cause of action. (CCP section 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has not alleged that Defendant owed her a duty. As Defendant correctly notes, Plaintiff is requesting that the court deduce that Defendant had a duty to inspect, warn, and ensure the existence of sufficient emergency response methods based off of the fact that the ride was held for the benefit of Defendant. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege the basis for Defendant's duty to inspect, warn, and ensure the existence of sufficient emergency response methods. The doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction does not apply to the case at hand. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
